verdict.verdict_rationale: Apple is a world-class business (profitability, cash generation, buyback discipline) trading at premium valuation (36.8x P/E, +29% vs peer median) with slowing growth (1.8% 3yr revenue CAGR) and high execution risk. Perfect EPS beat streak (11/11) masks buyback-dependent accretion, not organic momentum. DCF implies 48% downside, leaving no margin for error. Base case assumes 8-10% EPS growth through mix/buyback, multiple stays 35-36x, PT $325 (+6% upside). Bull case ($420, 25% prob) requires AI-driven iPhone acceleration + India greenfield + Services margin re-rate. Bear case ($220, 20% prob) hits on iPhone deceleration + China weakness + buyback cut. Risk/reward is balanced but skewed down; initiate 5% position on strength, add on 15-20% pullback to $260-280 for asymmetric 2-3yr return profile. Tim Cook's transition to John Ternus is a net positive (succession clarity, optionality on roadmap) but introduces execution risk. Recommend HOLD for current longs; BUY weakness on macro/multiple compression; avoid adding at current price.

NVIDIA is the foundational platform for a $3-4T annual AI infrastructure economy. Perfect execution (100% EPS beats, 4Q YoY acceleration), fortress balance sheet (0.07x debt/equity), and unmatched CUDA moat justify a BUY at 34x P/E—a 42% discount to peers despite superior growth and profitability. Vera CPU opens $200B new TAM; agentic AI inflection is structural, not cyclical. Risks (China lockout, TAM saturation, margin compression, custom silicon) are material but priced appropriately. Base case $330 PT (15-18% CAGR, 35-38x multiple) offers 47% upside; bull case $400 (Vera ramp + multiple expansion) offers 78% upside; bear case $210 (capex peak, custom silicon) offers downside to 6%. Position at 8% of concentrated portfolio—size reflects high conviction in agentic AI thesis but respect for execution and competitive risks.
